summary: Focused deterministic Go tests prove the binary-emitted contracts behind three reviewed Family B dev/skill claims: claim-review-input packets record a separate review budget for delegated autonomous continuation, refresh-plan packets record baseline commit, target policy, changed sources, carried-forward claims, stale evidence reasons, and dirty-working-tree treatment, and the improve proposal generator does not edit consumer-owned target files.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: dev/skill binary-emitted contracts that the Cautilus Agent reads (claim-review-input packet, refresh-plan packet, and improve proposal generator)
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: This bundle proves the deterministic binary-emitted shape that the Cautilus Agent depends on for delegated continuation budgets, refresh-plan reproducibility, and consumer-owned target safety. It does not prove that an actual skill execution selects refresh, proceeds within a delegated budget, or refuses to overwrite a consumer-owned file when an LLM is in the loop.
notClaimed: A live Cautilus Agent run was observed selecting refresh and recording the deterministic refresh-plan packet.
A live Cautilus Agent run was observed proceeding within a recorded review budget for delegated autonomous continuation.
A live improve runner was observed refusing to auto-edit consumer-owned prompts, skills, or instruction files when invoked end-to-end.
Every adapter-owned runner that wraps these binaries enforces the same boundaries; that remains the adapter's responsibility.

protectedBehaviors: claim-review-input packets emit a reviewBudget object recording the focused action bucket so a delegated autonomous-continuation budget is preserved in the packet, not hidden in operator memory
refresh-plan packets record the baseline gitCommit pulled from the previous claim packet plus the resolved target commit
refresh-plan packets record targetPolicy=current-head and workingTreePolicy=excluded so the deterministic dirty-working-tree treatment is visible to the skill
refresh-plan packets record changedSources, claimPlan lifecycle for carried-forward vs changed claims, and per-source changedClaimSources counts
refresh-plan packets record staleEvidence reasons when a previously verified evidence ref contentHash no longer matches
improve proposal generation carries target-file metadata and suggested changes without modifying the target file contents
improve proposals keep a baseline follow-up reference when no suggested change is produced, so a consumer-owned file is not silently rewritten

decision.reason: The deterministic Go tests prove the binary-emitted shape behind each Family B claim's constraint. A live Cautilus Agent execution would extend confidence to skill-level behavior but is not required to satisfy the binary-emitted packet/proposal constraints these claims encode.
